qa_pairs.question: Why are users encouraged to migrate to newer releases of Linux distributions?
qa_pairs.answer: Users are encouraged to migrate to newer releases to continue receiving patches and security updates.

qa_pairs.question: What happens to older images for Linux distributions that are past their End-of-Life (EOL)?
qa_pairs.answer: Older images for Linux distributions past their End-of-Life (EOL) will be removed.

qa_pairs.question: If an image for a Linux distribution is past its End-of-Life (EOL) and removed, can I still create new VMs with it?
qa_pairs.answer: No, if an image for a Linux distribution is past its End-of-Life (EOL) and removed, it will no longer be available when creating new VMs.

qa_pairs.question: Is it possible to continue running an existing VM with an End-of-Life (EOL) Linux distribution?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, it is possible to continue running an existing VM with an EOL Linux distribution, though it is not recommended.

qa_pairs.question: What is the consequence when a Linux distribution reaches its End-of-Life (EOL)?
qa_pairs.answer: After a Linux distribution reaches its End-of-Life (EOL), support and updates are no longer provided for it.

qa_pairs.question: What is the recommendation regarding using systems and platforms with older Linux distribution releases?
qa_pairs.answer: It is encouraged to migrate systems and platforms to newer releases to ensure ongoing patches and security updates.
